behaviors.description: Conversation history is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is list-slicing/order logic at a single function boundary (input history -> trimmed history), not an inter-module handoff bug. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks do not hide this; pure inputs can expose off-by-one/order bugs. LLM-DEP: No dependence on model output quality. MULTI-STEP: No agentic sequence. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (for raising above Unit): none concrete—if trimming is wrong, a pure function test fails directly.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure history-trimming function/object with >10 messages and assert exactly the last 10 are kept in correct chronological order, with boundary cases at 10 and 11 messages.

behaviors.description: Generated response addresses the customer's question rather than being generic or off-topic.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit/integration tests can verify prompt assembly and API wiring, but not answer relevance. REAL-INFRA: External model call is part of real behavior. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbed LLM outputs can always be made on-topic, hiding real off-topic failures. LLM-DEP: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment and non-deterministic output quality. MULTI-STEP: Single generation quality check, not a multi-agent pipeline. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): prompt builder unit test passes, yet real model still returns generic text. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): end-to-end with mocked LLM passes deterministic fixture, but production model drifts off-topic. REJECTION-GATE (reject Workflow): behavior text is one response quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real model on a representative set of support questions and evaluate whether each response directly answers the asked issue (with rubric/labeling and repeat runs to account for variance).

behaviors.description: Tone re-check identifies overly formal tone for casual-tier customers and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Logic wiring alone cannot prove tone classification correctness. REAL-INFRA: Real model behavior is needed for the tone judgment path. MOCK-HIDE: Mock tone-check responses can force 'too formal' and always pass trigger logic, hiding misclassification. LLM-DEP: 'Correctly identifies' is model-output quality dependent. MULTI-STEP: Includes regeneration trigger, but minimum real-risk boundary is the single model judgment that decides whether to re-prompt. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): branch logic unit test passes with forced flag=true, but real classifier misses formal tone. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): mocked tone checker + real orchestration passes, but real LLM tone check fails to flag. REJECTION-GATE (reject Workflow): behavior does not require validating quality of multiple generated outputs, only that tone detection decision causes regeneration.
behaviors.test_description: Use real tone-check model behavior on crafted formal replies for casual-tier context; assert the system flags them and performs a regeneration call in those cases.

behaviors.description: Full exchange (user message, initial response, regenerations) is persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure appears at module handoff (chat orchestration -> persistence layer/ORM schema), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: Requires module wiring and DB write/readback; local DB is sufficient (no real external network/OS primitive required beyond normal process I/O). MOCK-HIDE: Repository mocks can hide transaction/order/timestamp mapping bugs. LLM-DEP: No dependence on model quality; content can be fixed fixtures. MULTI-STEP: Not an agentic decision chain test; it is persistence integrity. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): serializer unit test passes, but ORM mapping drops regeneration rows or writes wrong timestamp field in integrated flow. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No required real external OS/network/subprocess boundary. (b) No AI output-quality assertion. (c) No multi-agent sequence requirement. (d) LLM stages are not under test; only persisted records are.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chat flow with deterministic stubbed responses including one regeneration, then query real local test DB and assert all records exist with expected fields and monotonic/accurate timestamps.

behaviors.description: If tone-triggered regeneration occurs, second response replaces first so customer never sees rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is orchestration/state handoff between regeneration pipeline and response presenter, not isolated function logic. REAL-INFRA: In-process module wiring is enough; no required real subprocess/network condition to expose core bug. MOCK-HIDE: Pure unit tests of selector function can miss integration timing/state bugs where first draft leaks to output layer. LLM-DEP: Behavior text conditions on trigger already occurring; it does not test model judgment quality itself. MULTI-STEP: Sequence exists, but minimum needed here is orchestration correctness once trigger=true, not evaluating multiple model decisions. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit): replacement helper returns second response in isolation, but integrated controller streams first response before replacement and leaks rejected draft. REJECTION-GATE (reject Agentic/Workflow): behavior explicitly starts with 'When the tone check triggers regeneration'—this disqualifies needing real model decision quality for this test.
behaviors.test_description: Run integrated request flow with forced tone-trigger path and two deterministic generated drafts; assert outbound customer payload/UI/API contains only draft #2 and draft #1 appears only in internal logs/audit data.
